title: Tapestry Canonical Object Model (the BOM)
description: The definitive, consumer-facing object model of the Tapestry game world — its bill of materials. The world is a labeled property graph: ENTITIES (nodes) connected by TYPED RELATIONS (edges), both carrying schemaless property bags. This schema formalizes the object types every other contract references: entities, the property-bag value types, kind/prototype inheritance, typed relations (with role/directionality/cardinality/exclusion-group/payload), and overlays (affects edges). C2 (portable state) and C4 (authoring) reference these shapes for entities, properties, relations, and overlays. This is a definitional schema: each $def describes the canonical shape of one object type.

id.description: An entity (node) identity: a monotonic, never-reused integer minted by the engine. Ids are stable for the life of a running session and grant O(1) access, but are NOT stable across sessions or instances. Durable cross-session identity is carried by aliases (authoring) and platform item ids (control plane), never by raw node ids.

alias.description: An author-facing stable string name for an entity. Authors address entities by alias; the engine maps each alias to a runtime id at load. The alias is the durable handle; the id is the runtime handle.

diceExpression.description: A dice-expression value: a parsed-once, rollable-many-times expression stored as its canonical string (e.g. "2d6+3", "1d20", "2d20kh1"). A property value type in its own right.

rollResult.description: A roll-result value: the structured outcome of evaluating a dice-expression. A property value type in its own right; serializes as this record so a roll is verifiable and presentable.

value.title: Property value (the variant system)
value.description: A single value in a property bag. The variant value types are: scalar (string/boolean/integer/float), identifier (a symbolic name), prose (literate text), code (an authored script body), link (a reference to an entity by id or alias), list (an ordered list of values), dice-expression, and roll-result. A structured object value (e.g. a cell position `{x,y,z}`) may also appear in an edge payload. JSON cannot distinguish identifier/prose/code from a plain string at the type level, so those three are carried as strings and disambiguated by the property's role in content; dice-expression is a string matched by form; roll-result is the structured record above. (anyOf — a structured object may match more than one branch.)

link.description: A link value: a typed reference to another entity, by runtime id or by author alias.

propertyBag.title: Property bag
propertyBag.description: An unbounded, schemaless map of property keys to values. Carried by both entities (the node's own state, conceptually its reflexive self-edge) and relations (edge payloads). Any key is permitted; values follow the variant system.

lifecycle.description: An entity's lifecycle state. Entities are never hard-deleted — they transition lifecycle state and are referenceable forever. Perception/scope queries gate on this state (a destroyed item is known-about but not perceived; a corpse is still seen).

entity.title: Entity (node)
entity.description: The fundamental object: a node in the world graph. Carries an identity (id and/or alias), a property bag (its own state), an optional kind/prototype it delegates to, and a lifecycle state.

id.description: Runtime id; null before a graph is materialized/lowered.

kind.description: The prototype this entity delegates to (an `is-a` relationship). `kind` is sugar for `parent`: both name a prototype entity from which this one inherits properties and behaviour.

parent.description: Explicit prototype override. Resolution order: explicit parent > kind > default > root.

properties.description: The entity's own property bag (its self-edge payload).

effectiveEntity.title: Effective entity (materialized view)
effectiveEntity.description: An entity with its property bag FOLDED over its prototype (kind/parent) chain plus any active overlays — the materialized, effective view. Own properties layer on inherited ones; overlays then fold by precedence (see overlay). This is what perception and rules read.

role.description: The semantic role of a relation type, which determines how the engine's spatial/perception machinery treats it. none = a plain content relation the spatial walk ignores; containment = in-ness (location group); support = on-ness (location group); incorporation = part-of (structural, OUTSIDE the location exclusion group); map = room adjacency; portal = a passage/door between containers; sensory = a perception channel (see/touch/hear/smell/know).

sense.description: A sensory sub-channel, for relations and overlays with role=sensory. `know` is non-spatial (knowledge as a sense).

cardinality.description: The cardinality of a relation type, expressed as (source side) × (target side), each `one` or `various`. The `one` side is a single slot (setting a new edge replaces the old — that IS the move); the `various` side is an ordered set.

directionality.description: Whether the relation is directed (from → to) or undirected.

symmetry.description: A symmetric relation auto-maintains its reverse edge; an asymmetric one does not.

relationType.title: Relation type (edge schema)
relationType.description: A first-class, declarable edge schema. The engine ships a built-in set (the location group in/on/carried/worn, plus map, support, incorporation/part-of, sensory channels, and has-seen); content declares more (factions, knowledge, quests). Roles are content-assignable.

exclusion_group.description: Optional mutual-exclusion group. Relations sharing a group are mutually exclusive on a node: the location group {in, on, carried, worn} is various-to-one ACROSS the group (a thing is in exactly one of them; setting one auto-evicts the others).

invariants.description: Structural invariants enforced at edge-write. Acyclicity applies to containment/support/incorporation.

payload_schema.description: Optional shape of this relation's edge payload.

relation.title: Relation (edge)
relation.description: A typed, directed edge between two entities, with an optional property bag (payload). Structural invariants (cardinality, exclusion group, acyclicity) are enforced when the edge is written.

from.description: Source entity, by id or alias.

to.description: Target entity, by id or alias.

payload.description: Edge payload. For a location-group edge this carries the entity's integer cell position `{x,y,z}` within its container (position is optional; absent or 0,0,0 for most entities).

cell.title: Cell position
cell.description: An integer cell coordinate within a container, carried on a location-group edge payload. Integers only — never floats (floats would break deterministic ordering).

overlayOp.description: The declarative operation an overlay applies to a property. The closed op-algebra the engine folds without knowing content meaning. A modification needing arbitrary computation is NOT an overlay — it is a trigger that writes a property.

overlay.title: Overlay (affects edge)
overlay.description: An effect/modifier expressed as an `affects(source → target)` edge. Conditions, materials, equipment bonuses, auras, and spell effects all express as overlays. Effective properties are computed by folding the entity's base property bag with all inbound affects edges in precedence order.

source.description: The entity the effect originates from (the caster, the worn item, an aura node), by id or alias.

target.description: The entity the effect applies to, by id or alias.

property.description: The property key the overlay modifies (e.g. "light", "attack_bonus", "size").

precedence.description: Fold order relative to other overlays on the same property.

duration.description: Remaining ticks; null/absent for an indefinite effect. The engine decrements it and emits an expiry event when it elapses.

title_note: Top-level: this schema is referenced by $def. A document conforming to this schema is any one of the canonical object types.
